flags.flag: Contact Center / David Lim — 47 responses cite management behaviour. Sentiment avg 1.9. Urgent review recommended.
Retail early-tenure churn — 100.0% of Retail exits occur before 2 years. Onboarding or scheduling friction likely.
Tech & Sales compensation gap — Compensation mentioned in 51.1% of Tech and Sales exits. Benchmarking review overdue.
Marketing burnout signal — Workload and burnout themes spike at 2-5 year tenure band in Marketing.

verbatim_quotes.text: To be honest I liked the company and the product. But I cannot overstate how toxic the team environment was. I came home exhausted every day — not from the work but from the management. I had to choose my health.

I have genuinely valued my time here. The culture is strong, the leadership is decent, and my manager has been a real advocate for my development. I am leaving for a specialist role that aligns better with where I want to take my career long term.

I want to be clear that I am not leaving because of the company culture or my manager — both are good. I am leaving because the workload has been unmanageable for over a year and I do not see it changing. I need to find a role where I can actually switch off.
